name: Voyager Technologies, Inc./TX
business: Voyager Technologies is a defense technology and space solutions company that designs, develops, and operates advanced systems for national security, space exploration, and commercial space markets. Its capabilities include communications and intelligence systems, missile-defense technologies, propulsion and energetics, in-space infrastructure, and end-to-end space mission services. The company serves U.S. government agencies and commercial/international customers across three segments: Defense & National Security, Space Solutions, and Starlab Space Stations.
sellsTo: U.S. Government (including DoD, NASA, Missile Defense Agency)
Defense primes (e.g., Lockheed Martin, RTX Corporation, Millennium Space Systems)

exposures.sentence: We provide controllable solid propulsion technology that enables missile programs critical to national defense.
We believe our end-to-end intelligence analytics platform enhances how government agencies collect, process and act on critical intelligence.

We offer space-qualified radiation-hardened laser and radio frequency (RF) communications systems and advanced electro-optical and digital systems, including transceivers, mission-data transmitters and command and data handling systems.

We design and produce critical technologies for controlling and navigating spacecraft, including sun sensors, star trackers, space cameras and inertial measurement units serving the satellite constellation market and other demanding missions.
We are developing AI-enabled edge computing in space, processing data directly at the source to eliminate ground transmission latency and reduce vulnerabilities in contested environments.
We provide advanced space maneuver and in-space mobility capabilities that enable spacecraft to operate in congested and contested orbital environments.
The Bishop Airlock is the first and only permanent, privately-owned module attached to the ISS, enabling movement of equipment, supplies and payloads between the ISS and open space.
We are the largest commercial user of the ISS in the world.

Starlab JV is a Voyager-led and majority-owned global joint venture designing and building Starlab, which is expected to serve as a commercial replacement for the ISS following its planned decommissioning in 2030.
Our solid fuel systems deliver precise thrust for steering and positioning, including the high-performance requirements for hypersonic applications.
Through our integration of Estes Energetics, we have expanded beyond propulsion subsystems into vertically integrated energetics manufacturing.

In August 2025, we acquired 100% of the equity ownership of ElectroMagnetic Systems, Inc. ( EMSI ) which is a premier developer of artificial intelligence ( AI ) and machine learning-based automated target recognition software and intelligence analytics for space-based radar systems.

catalysts.sentence: We currently estimate the cost to design, manufacture and launch Starlab to be approximately $2.8 billion to $3.3 billion, with launch anticipated in 2029 and revenue generation expected in Starlab’s first full year of operation.
Milestone payments are expected to be earned through April 2026.
